office_does: One of two House seats for the 37th District (southeast Seattle, Skyway, part of Renton), voting on state law, budget, and taxes.
race_blurb: An open seat vacated by Rep. Chipalo Street's Senate run. Nonprofit executive Jaelynn Scott (D), heavily endorsed by Democratic officials, faces first-time Independent candidate Evon McCorkle, a college student with pamphlet-only evidence; both advance to the general.

candidates.summary: Executive Director of the Lavender Rights Project and community organizer with an extensive endorsement slate; would be the first openly transgender person to serve in the Washington Legislature.
highlights: Says she built a coalition that redirected $16 million from jail expansion into supportive housing and alternative public-safety approaches [S1][S2]

Platform includes 'compassionate public safety' (behavioral-health responders over police), universal healthcare, universal childcare, and progressive taxation on wealthy residents and corporations [S1][S2]
Endorsements span Rep. Pramila Jayapal, Sen. Bernie Sanders, King County Executive Girmay Zahilay, and numerous local Democratic officials [S1][S4]

taxes.basis: Calls for progressive taxation on wealthy residents and corporations and expanded cash assistance like the Working Families Tax Credit.

spending.basis: Platform of universal childcare, publicly funded preschool, and expanded cash assistance treats public investment as the primary lever.

housing.basis: Wants to expand affordable and permanent supportive housing on the Chief Seattle Club model, with a record of redirecting jail funds into supportive housing.

safety.basis: 'Compassionate public safety': behavioral-health professionals instead of police for crises, violence interruption funding, police accountability, and the $16M jail-to-housing redirection.

experience.basis: No prior elected office — an outsider by tenure — though with nonprofit executive leadership and a concrete claimed policy outcome.

immigration.basis: Supports limits on surveillance technology and data sharing with federal agencies.

healthcare.basis: Supports universal healthcare including vision, dental, and mental health coverage.

social.basis: Leads a nonprofit for Black gender-diverse communities and platforms protecting Medicaid, reproductive, and gender-affirming care.

tech.basis: Supports limits on surveillance technology and data sharing — a regulatory posture toward tech.

candidates.summary: College student studying mathematics and physics and first-time candidate preferring the Independent Party; a lifelong district resident running on education access, housing stability, and restoring trust in government.
highlights: First-time candidate with no prior elected experience; states he is 'not backed by powerful insiders or political machines' [S1]
Lead priorities are education access, affordable housing and housing stability, and community safety [S1]
Evidence rests on the pamphlet alone: his campaign website was unreachable and no substantive news coverage was found [S1]

experience.basis: College student and first-time candidate with no prior elected experience, running as an outsider.

reform.basis: Frames his candidacy around restoring trust between residents and officials and being 'not backed by powerful insiders or political machines.'
